title: China’s Closest Island to Taiwan Is a Monument to Reunification
gist: China's Pingtan Island, its closest point to Taiwan, has been developed by the tourism industry into a monument to eventual reunification, complete with a 'Beijing-Taipei Highway' that on Chinese map apps appears to cross the sea to Taiwan.

insights.claim: China has developed Pingtan Island in Fujian as a tourism site touted as 'the closest point to Taiwan' and covered it in monuments to an eventual reunification with the island Beijing claims.
insights.speaker: Will Wain-Williams
insights.so_what: Reflects Beijing's investment in normalizing reunification through soft-power infrastructure, a barometer of political intent.
insights.locator: Unlike other islands along Fujian's coast

insights.claim: The Beijing-Taipei Highway ends at the small fishing island of Pingtan, yet Chinese map apps show the road crossing the sea all the way to Taiwan.
insights.speaker: Will Wain-Williams
insights.so_what: State cartography encoding a claimed future annexation illustrates how deeply reunification is embedded in Chinese public messaging.
insights.quote: This road will lead all the way to Taipei
insights.locator: “This road will lead all the way to Taipei”
